boundary: Only accepted observable trace artifacts should be normalized. Do not normalize protected/internal material, unredacted regulated data, source code, model weights, private prompts, hidden activations, or private chain-of-thought.
claim_boundary: Trace schema only. It does not create legal advice, financial advice, binding terms, compliance certification, production validation, automated verdicts, free-pilot commitments, autonomous client action, truth validation, or hidden-mechanism claims.

fields.description: Stable trace, case, record, workflow, ticket, or generated row identifier.
Observable event time, sequence time, or ordered step marker.
Visible actor, system, service, component, or pseudonymized identifier.
Visible workflow step, action, event type, operation, or state transition.
Visible input, request, trigger, context, or input summary if available.
Visible output, response, result, or claimed result if available.
Claimed final state, status, completion state, ledger/payment state, or endpoint if available.
Source row, log reference, trace reference, or supporting observable record.
Limitations, review notes, redaction notes, or other bounded context.

purpose: Standard review shape for accepted buyer-exported observable traces.
